```json
[
  {
    "idea": "Unemployment Claims Volatility Spike Reversal Trade",
    "family": "labor_jobs",
    "data": {
      "source": "FRED series ICSA (Initial Claims weekly) via fred_series adapter",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "When weekly initial jobless claims spike unexpectedly above their 4-week moving average by more than 15%, labor market shock creates mean-reversion pressure. Claims typically normalize within 2-4 weeks unless structural deterioration is occurring.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLF",
      "rationale": "Claims spikes signal potential Fed rate-cut expectations and flight-to-safety rotation into financials as bond yields compress."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "Initial claims breach 4-week MA by 15%+ on weekly close; enter long XLF",
      "exit": "Claims return within 5% of 4-week MA OR after 3 weeks, whichever comes first",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "monthly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "FRED jobless claims data updates weekly; volatility spikes occur monthly on seasonal and unexpected labor market moves; reversion typically completes within 21 days."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Natural Gas Futures Volatility Pump Rotation",
    "family": "macro_input_pressure",
    "data": {
      "source": "EIA Natural Gas Storage Weekly (via html_table adapter) paired with price_only daily futures proxy",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Unusual weekly EIA storage draws (>10% above seasonal norm) combined with rising futures volatility trigger energy sector rotation; energy stocks often lag futures by 2-5 trading days due to portfolio rebalancing.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLE",
      "rationale": "Input cost shocks typically compress energy sector valuations but benefit large integrated producers on margin expansion when commodities spike."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "EIA weekly storage draw >10% above 5-year seasonal median; natural gas futures up 2%+ on day of release; enter long XLE at close",
      "exit": "After 10 trading days OR once natural gas futures reverse 3% from entry, whichever first",
      "expected_frequency": "high"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "EIA releases storage weekly; abnormal draws occur 3-4 times per quarter; futures volatility spikes regularly; price action lags trigger by defined window."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Regional Earthquake Cluster Insurance Demand Spike",
    "family": "local_economy_weirdness",

      "source": "USGS earthquake_activity daily feed (magnitude 3.5+) via earthquake_activity adapter; paired with Google Trends searches for 'earthquake insurance' and 'home inspection'",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Clusters of 4+ earthquakes in 7 days (any US region, magnitude 3.5+) trigger 40-60% surge in insurance/inspection searches within 48 hours. Real estate and property stocks (builders, insurers) experience brief bullish pressure as emergency repair demand rises.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLRE",
      "rationale": "Earthquake clusters drive near-term demand for property inspections, repairs, and insurance policy uptake; benefits regional real estate and construction activity."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "4+ magnitude 3.5+ earthquakes within 7 days in same US region; Google Trends 'earthquake insurance' index rises 40%+ week-over-week; enter long XLRE",
      "exit": "After 5 trading days OR Google Trends 'earthquake insurance' returns to baseline, whichever first",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "monthly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "medium",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "USGS earthquake data is continuous; regional clusters occur 2-3 times per quarter; Google Trends lags by 1-2 days; 5-day position window ensures frequent fire opportunities."
  },
  {
    "idea": "EV Charger Buildout Acceleration Signal",
    "family": "macro_input_pressure",
    "data": {
      "source": "OpenChargeMap total charger count (daily via openchargemap adapter) paired with price_only XLI daily momentum",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "When weekly net new charger additions exceed prior 8-week rolling average by 20%+, it signals infrastructure investment acceleration. Industrial and energy stocks respond bullishly within 3-7 days as construction/manufacturing demand rises.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLI",
      "rationale": "Charger buildout expansion signals sustained capex cycle in industrial manufacturing, electrical equipment, and construction services."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "Weekly net charger adds (vs 8-week rolling avg) spike 20%+; XLI volume above 90th percentile; enter long XLI at next day's open",
      "exit": "After 7 trading days OR if weekly charger growth rate drops below 5%, whichever first",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",

    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "OpenChargeMap updates daily; charger deployment accelerations occur monthly; industrial sector responds predictably to capex cycle signals."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Freight Recession Bounce-Back Signal",
    "family": "freight_logistics",
    "data": {
      "source": "FRED series TRFVOLUSM157N (Truck freight volume monthly) via fred_series adapter",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "When monthly truck freight volume rebounds 3%+ from its lowest point in a 6-month window, it signals logistics demand recovery. Industrial and consumer discretionary sectors experience 2-4 week rallies as inventory replenishment accelerates.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLI",
      "rationale": "Freight recovery is a leading indicator of production and consumption rebound; benefits industrial equipment and logistics operators."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "Truck freight volume rebounds 3%+ month-over-month from trailing 6-month minimum; enter long XLI on release day close",
      "exit": "After 14 trading days OR if truck freight declines 1%+ in next month's report, whichever first",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "monthly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "medium",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "FRED truck freight updates monthly; rebounding cycles occur every 2-3 months during normal business cycles; 14-day exit window ensures capture of mean-reversion momentum."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Bank Stress Premium Decompression Fade",
    "family": "consumer_stress",
    "data": {
      "source": "FRED series MMNRNJ (Money Market Mutual Fund Shares weekly) and price_only XLF daily volatility via fred_series and price_only adapters",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "When money market fund inflows spike (new deposits into MMNRNJ) by 15%+ week-over-week during periods of elevated VIX, it signals panic flight-to-safety. This excess liquidity typically reverts as fear normalizes, creating 5-10 day bullish fade trades in financial equities.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLF",
      "rationale": "Money market inflows during stress compress funding spreads and reduce deposit competition for banks; stabilization allows equities to re-rate upward."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "MMNRNJ weekly inflow >15% above 4-week avg; VIX >25; XLF 20-day volatility >25%; enter long XLF",
      "exit": "After 10 trading days OR if VIX drops 30%+ from entry level, whichever first",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "monthly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "FRED money market data updates weekly; stress events occur monthly; VIX spikes provide clear entry windows; panic reversions are typically 7-14 day events."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Airline Load Factor Capacity Utilization Pop",
    "family": "travel_mobility",
    "data": {
      "source": "BTS Airline Load Factor (monthly via bts_airline_load_factor adapter) and XLY price_only daily",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "When reported airline load factors exceed 85% (monthly BTS release), it signals demand strength. Consumer discretionary and hospitality stocks rally 3-7 days after release as investor sentiment shifts toward travel and leisure demand confidence.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLY",
      "rationale": "High airline load factors indicate strong leisure/business travel demand, benefiting consumer discretionary spending and hospitality valuations."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "BTS monthly load factor >85%; enter long XLY one trading day after release",
      "exit": "After 7 trading days OR if next month's load factor prints <82%, whichever first",
      "expected_frequency": "high"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "monthly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "medium",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "BTS releases load factor data monthly; capacity is consistently 83-87% in normal markets; seasonality creates predictable cycles; 7-day window captures post-release momentum."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Political Insider Buying Conviction Clusters",
    "family": "political_insider_filing",
    "data": {
      "source": "SEC insider trading filings (Form 4 aggregate buys per sector, weekly via html_table adapter scrape of SEC EDGAR filings)",
      "type": "scrape",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "When insider buy volume (Form 4 filings) in a single sector exceeds prior 3-month average by 40%+ within a rolling 5-day window, it signals conviction-level buying by corporate officers. Equities typically outperform sector benchmarks by 2-5% in following 2-3 weeks as momentum builds.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLK",
      "rationale": "Concentrated insider buying in technology reflects executive conviction on valuation; historically precedes 15-30 day outperformance rallies."
    },

      "entry": "Aggregate Form 4 buy volume in XLK exceeds 3-month rolling avg by 40%+; enter long XLK at next day open",
      "exit": "After 14 trading days OR if insider buy volume returns to baseline, whichever first",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "SEC filings update daily and aggregate weekly; insider conviction clusters occur every 2-3 weeks across sectors; 14-day windows align with insider position accumulation cycles."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Utility Volatility Compression Reversion Bounce",
    "family": "macro_input_pressure",
    "data": {
      "source": "XLU price_only daily (volatility, drawdown, momentum via price_only adapter)",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "When XLU 20-day realized volatility drops below 10% (multi-year low for defensive sector), and the ETF declines 3%+ from recent 20-day high, mean-reversion bias favors 5-10 day bullish rebounds as fear premiums collapse and dividend yields reactivate portfolio flows.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLU",
      "rationale": "Utilities compress during market risk-off; low volatility + small declines create mechanical dividend-capture and rotation-back-to-safety setups."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "XLU 20-day volatility <10%; XLU down 3%+ from 20-day high; enter long XLU",
      "exit": "After 8 trading days OR if XLU 20-day volatility rises above 15%, whichever first",
      "expected_frequency": "high"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Volatility compression in XLU occurs monthly; 3% declines are routine in market turbulence; 8-day windows capture reversion peaks; setup fires 1-2x per month."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Gold Price Spike Safe-Haven Equities Lag Rotation",
    "family": "attention_sentiment",
    "data": {
      "source": "Price_only Gold Futures (GC=F via Yahoo Finance) daily paired with XLV daily price and volume",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "When gold futures rally 2%+ on a single day (fear/flight-to-safety signal), defensive healthcare stocks (XLV) typically lag by 1-3 trading days before following. This creates a 3-5 day tactical positioning opportunity where healthcare outperforms once sentiment stabilizes.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLV",
      "rationale": "Gold spikes signal macro uncertainty; healthcare rotations follow once investors recognize stability; timing gap creates 4-8% tactical outperformance windows."
